nfrs.brief: Existing bugs and their data survive every schema migration with no loss; each iteration's model change preserves the bugs filed before it
A bug's status is always one of the recognized states; an unrecognized status is rejected at write time, never stored
Error messages are clear and specific, naming the offending value rather than generic 'bad request' responses
New tables introduced by a migration are additive where possible; old reads keep working during rollout
Bug data is durably persisted to the database on successful form submission
